composers.bio_summary: Originally employed at Sega, Masayuki Nagao was preparing to take a new job at Opus Corp., resulting in the company receiving a "Sound Special Thanks" credit.

style_traits.signature: Minor-key melodic writing, stepwise bass lines, high rhythmic density
Driving rock-influenced rhythms, modal harmonic language, prominent bass

composers.bio_summary: One of the few Sega of Japan employees to confirm Jackson's involvement in Sonic the Hedgehog 3 .
Tasked with creating Sonic the Hedgehog 3' s music driver.

style_traits.notes: Special Stage music recycled from SegaSonic Bros.

composers.bio_summary: Composer and one of Michael Jackson's closest professional collaborators at the time. One of three members of Jackson's sound team to confirm the artist's involvement in the project.

style_traits.signature: IceCap Zone Act 1 (based on 'Hard Times' by The Jetzons)
style_traits.notes: Michael Jackson associate; contributed to Sonic 3

composers.bio_summary: One of three members of Jackson's sound team to confirm the artist's involvement in the project.

Responsible for managing the game's sound development, which entailed assigning Sega staff to production work and contracting outside composers; he confirms he did not compose for the soundtrack itself.
One of the few Sega of Japan employees to confirm Jackson's involvement in Sonic the Hedgehog 3 .
Two jingles previously composed by Yukifumi Makino for the original Sonic the Hedgehog were included in Sonic the Hedgehog 3 and Sonic & Knuckles .
Credited under Cube Corp., the sound production company she was contracted through. Between her and fellow Cube composer Masanori Hikichi, they created a total of eight tracks for the project.
Credited under Cube Corp., the sound production company he was contracted through. Between him and fellow Cube composer Miyoko Takaoka, they created a total of eight tracks for the project.
Received a "Sound Special Thanks" for an unknown contribution.

An American pop music artist who became involved in the composition of Sonic the Hedgehog 3' s soundtrack relatively late in development, bringing a number of members of his personal sound team to the project.
One of three members of Jackson's sound team to confirm the artist's involvement in the project.
Michael Jackson's long-time Japanese coordinator, responsible for assisting the artist while working in the country. Receives a "special thanks" credit.

Worked alongside bassist Damon Doiron, drummer Steve Golladay, and keyboardist Brad Buxer as guitarist and vocalist for new wave band The Jetzons . Part of a composition he worked on with Buxer, "Hard Times," was the basis for Ice Cap Zone's theme.

STI's in-house composer. Recalls being called into the project last minute to create several jingles for Sonic 3 , most of which would go unused until the release of Sonic & Knuckles . Received a "Sound Special Thanks" in Sonic 3' s credits.

Responsible for both hiring and firing Jackson from the project, as well as assigning Howard Drossin to the project late in development. Hector was the first person within Sega to confirm Jackson's involvement.
Coordinator

Sega of Japan executive, director, and engineer. The Sega representative who worked most closely with Michael Jackson during his past collaborations with the company. Suzuki was Jackson's primary point of contact for Sega, and the two reputedly developed a close friendship over the years.
Ported the music of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 and Sonic & Knuckles to the Sonic & Knuckles Collection.
Ported the music of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 and Sonic & Knuckles to the Sonic & Knuckles Collection.
Ported the music of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 and Sonic & Knuckles to the Sonic & Knuckles Collection.

tracks.title: Hidden Palace Zone / Lava Reef Act 2

drivers.notes: Custom variant of SMPS written for S3K by Yoshiaki 'Milpo' Kashima.
